headline: CME Group to Launch Beef Trim Contracts to Manage Risk Across the Cattle Supply Chain
summary: CME Group, the world's leading derivatives marketplace, today announced plans to launch two types of Beef Trim contracts, which will help the agriculture industry manage risk associated with the key ingredient in producing hamburgers. Trading is expected to start on July 20, 2026, pending regulatory review.

headline: CME Group to Launch Single Stock Futures on July 27
summary: CME Group, the world's leading derivatives marketplace, today announced it will launch Single Stock futures across more than 50 of the top U.S. stocks on July 27, pending completion of all regulatory review and processes. This new offering will include 55 larger-sized and 22 Micro-sized futures contracts, providing market participants with additional flexibility to manage their equity exposure.

headline: Gold’s rebound has a problem. Here’s what happens next
summary: Gold finally mounted a comeback on Friday, June 26, but the rebound didn’t erase the bigger warning in the market. Spot gold rose 1.2% to $4,073.78 an ounce, while August U.S. gold futures climbed 1.2% to $4,096.30, according to CNBC. The move came just as the dollar eased off from recent ...
